title: Delete Node in a Linked List O(1)

content.problemStatement: Write a function to delete a node in a singly linked list. You will not be given access to the head of the list instead, you will be given access to the node to be deleted directly. It is guaranteed that the node to be deleted is not a tail node in the list.
constraints: 1 <= number of nodes in the linked list <= 10^5
The node to be deleted is guaranteed to be not the tail node in the list.
content.inputFormat: The function takes a ListNode as input, which is the node to be deleted from the linked list.
content.outputFormat: The function does not return any value. It modifies the linked list in-place by deleting the given node.

solution.approach: To delete a node in a singly linked list without access to the head, we can copy the value of the next node into the current node and then bypass the next node.
